continuous_token.delta_history: previous formal window within the same clean segment

supervision.idle_target: window has zero sample overlap with every retained MI event
supervision.mi_target: window is an evaluator-eligible first-commit point and uses that event class
supervision.ignored_target: window overlaps an MI event but is not evaluator-eligible
supervision.outer_subject_target_flow: truth inventory may be opened centrally, but outer subject sequences and labels are excluded from every training and threshold-selection object and are used only by the final evaluator

model.outputs: one TASK logit plus four conditional MI-class logits

loss.state_component: equal mean of IDLE and TASK binary NLL
loss.class_component: equal mean of the four per-class conditional NLL values

training.batch_semantics: all padded clean segments in the split form one gradient step and must fit below batch_size
training.determinism: decision seed fixes initialization and deterministic CUDA algorithms

nested_loso.outer: leave one subject out for final evaluation
nested_loso.inner: among the remaining eight, train seven and validate one
nested_loso.shared_pair_optimization: one seven-subject trajectory per unordered held pair; each endpoint permanently freezes its checkpoint when its own patience is first reached
nested_loso.final_epoch_rule: median of eight inner best epochs rounded half up

decision_shell.ready_submit: emit argmax conditional MI class when GRU P(TASK) reaches selected commit threshold
decision_shell.wait_idle_reset: return to READY when GRU P(IDLE) reaches selected reset threshold
decision_shell.duplicate_prevention: WAIT_IDLE never emits an MI command

threshold_selection.feasibility: equal-subject mean validation FAR must not exceed anchor mean FAR

aggregation: equal-subject macro within paired seed, then mean and population standard deviation over seeds
claim_limit: session0 OOF nested-subject evaluation of a continuous learned decision layer; GRU owns task evidence, MI class and idle reset evidence, while READY/WAIT_IDLE is retained only as a duplicate-command safety shell; not session1 performance or deployed artifact handling
